text.title: Plausible Event Tracking
text.summary: Custom events, goal tracking, CSS class events, and revenue tracking patterns.

rule.statement: Define goals in the Plausible dashboard before firing events from code — events that do not match a defined goal are silently ignored.
rule.rationale: Plausible requires goals to be pre-registered in the dashboard settings. Firing events without corresponding goal definitions produces no data in the dashboard.

rule.statement: Use CSS class tagged events for CTA click tracking when possible — no JavaScript code changes needed when adding new buttons.
rule.rationale: Tagged events reduce deployment friction: a designer can add a tracking class in HTML without touching JavaScript. This is also more resilient to JS bundle changes.

example.code: View Pricing

rule.statement: Verify the Plausible plan supports revenue tracking before implementing — this feature is only available in the hosted Business plan, not in Community Edition (CE).
rule.rationale: Revenue tracking silently fails on plans that do not support it. Verifying the plan before implementation avoids lost revenue data without error messages.
